channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 53 replies — 212.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:13 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62402991
>>62402983
Yeah he's right if you have enough capital you can easily live off it. The problem is morons with 10k thinking VOO is going to make them rich after investing for a few years.

62402995
>>62402991
no he isn't idiot

62403030
>>62402931
The average person is pretty poor. And this is just braindead fire and forget index fund investing. 100 or 120 per month gives you 200k in 40 years.
You can put in 500 or 600 per month if you want a million in 40 years. Of course it won't be a million, it will be more like 5 million or 10 million due to hyper inflation, but it will be equal to what a million is today.

62403034
>>62402981
ETFs are how you make generational wealth without thinking. It literally requires no work. Picking stocks burns brainpower and also has a high failure rate.

62403044
>>62402991
lmao no, etfs are good for retirement as a middle class wagecuck; they wont make you rich sure but to claim is only useful for millionaires is bullshit.
Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this.

62403053
>>62403044
The median 64 year old american has 400k to retire on and they seem to be ok with it.
That's what middle class means in america. Of course 1 in 10 Americans have a net worth of 1 million, but that's not controlled for age.

62403140
>>62400636
Im the second one.
Yeehaw

62403184
>>62403140
same

62403215
>>62403030
>The average person is pretty poor.

Kind of.

>The U.S. median household income is $83,730. For individual personal income, the median figure is $45,140. -- St. Louis FED

Most Americans can afford to save more than $100/month, especially if they are married or have a SO, but most Americans really don't think about the future, so they finance the best lifestyle the bank is willing to finance instead of living well beneath their means. Most Americans should not be going to Disneyworld or on vacations every year, especially on credit, but they do and this leads them to get into debt, making it "impossible" to save money.

I try to save as much as possible because no way am I not going to settle for $200k in the year of our Lord 2060. That's broke money today, much less in 40 years when the USD is further in the toilet.

62404485
>>62400560
I want to be free. The colour of my bugatti is meat colored

62404617
>>62400493
it's over

62404649
>>62400515
And jewish

62404732
>>62400493
Im all in on aussie etf covering nasdaq NDQ, 60k dollars so far. But will probably start getting other stuff to be safe. I figured its fine if it goes down 50% at this early stage cause 30k isnt world ending

62404755
>>62400493
forget VOO and forget QQQ. AVUV and AVDV is where you should be, mix in some VTI and VEU so you don't kill yourself. Whatever that threshold is for you.

62404820
>>62404755
look at this shit, Small cap value beats its market by 3% consistently over long periods, doesn't matter if its US or international. QQQ outperforming in the last 10 years is a tiny sample and not significant. Don't trust it, its a mirage. You're a boglehead trust the statistics, numbers don't lie.

62404829
>>62404820
Yeah QQQ is simply doing good due to a highly concentrated growth period
This is interesting though is there any catch or downside to discuss with small cap? Why exactly is it consistently better?
I got $55000 in VOO and some in SCHD…….Man this is making me realize when I first started I put $5000 into SCHD before realizing chasing dividends at age 26 is pointless

62404895
>>62404829
the catch is that its not very correlated with the broader market and its more volatile (more risk means more return, and in this case its actually true. Most of the time when you see people in /smg/ talk about more risk its not actually coming with more quantifiable return. More risk doesn't magically make more return), so if you are 100% SCV there may be stretches where you have to watch VOO have solid returns while you get nothing and kill yourself, but the periods when SCV beats the market it kills.

As for why it beats the market. You will have to read farma and french's research papers, the short of it is that there are quantifiable metrics which can explain stock outperformance, they are called 5 factors and they are Momentum, Size, Value, Profitability and Volatility. Small cap value stocks overlap positively with the most factors.

62404949
>>62404755
I just opened a new stock account strictly for etf investing.
I’m thinking of doing 2 shares of AVUV and 2 shares of AVDV each month indefinitely. (1 share each week alternating between the two).
Wish me luck.

62405063
The S&P 500 is more crash proof now just because so many Boomers rely on it for their 401ks and Social Security is only 5 years away from being completely depleted. If we went through a lost decade like the 2000s there would be Great Depression levels of disparity as the biggest consumer class would be gone. Given how they responded to the 2008 crisis and Covid the powers will keep stocks propped up no matter the cost

62405112
>>62400493
wait until this current bear routing is over. Right now you get nothing but declines for the foreseeable future. But that will bottom out then it can resume and your philosophy works

62405201
>>62400554
notice how whenever you call out a boomer fund or jewish 401k, they sperg out and start insulting you and calling you stupid, gee great way to sell your "product"

62405226
>>62405112
Yeah, I got time and will (hopefully) only be making more money for when prices drop too
Don’t wanna retire at 65 but wouldn’t mind 40/45 if I can hit 150-200k invested soon

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

item.title: Polkadot update — 30 replies — 115.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-24 18:58 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

62401739
>>62401689
Interesting
Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877
>>62401739
A lambo will be in your future

62401946
>>62401877
Checked.

62402307
>>62399251
You have a missed call

62402317
>>62399190
> imagine thinking we need another token to fix polkadot's identity crisis

dot works fine as gas, just let it be the jam fuel and move on.

62402326
>>62402317
Didn't NEO have a similar issue

62402419
>>62402317
it's just an nft with price discovery so they don't have to do all of that coretime auction bullshit again

62403350
>>62402317

Thats not even what its use is for

62403448
>>62402317

im surprised dot isnt trading at 10 dollars considering the pedigree

but they overspent on marketing during a bear market instead of 2021. but on the plus side the dot treasury has started reporting quarterly profits. Unlike Cardano or Chainlink

it wont stay under a dollar for long

62404229
>>62403448
>it wont stay under a dollar for long

Under 50 cents by 2027

62404438
0.87 new low lmao. god what a piece of shit.

62404588
15k @ 7$.
I dont care

62404599
>>62404229
It'll go to 0. Nobody cares; it's a useless chain that serves to inflate Gayvin's ego, making him believe he's intelligent and respected. This desperate propaganda on /biz/ shows how dead this project is.

62405376
>>62401877
Checked
I've heard that before
We'll see
Been waiting for an actual parallel economy system for fucking ever
Was watching komodo and loki/oxen before this and they never got off the ground

62405404
Depression

62405433
Didn't read but is he hugging DOT and shilling a new token?

62406887
I bought more

item.title: MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking? — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking?

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:11 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62405530
why would i panic when i'm not holding any crapto?

62405594
I hold the real deal not the proxy.

62405608
My fiat bags are ready to slurp the $20k bottom in October

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405631
/biz/ is smart money and rotated to semi-conductors and data centers.

62405650
>>62405528
I was tempted to buy STRC a month ago lmao. Dodged a bullet.

62406041
I'm getting greedy here.
If you cut through the noise, their balance sheet is extremely strong. Even if BTC drops quite a bit further.
There are also contradictory fears at play here. The main fear is that Strategy will start selling huge amounts of BTC into a massive downward spiral to serve the dividends. But if that is the case, the dividend is safe, and thus STRC not falling to $0, right? On the other hand, if they rugpull STRC, "thanks for the money we're suspending the dividend forever", then the market doesn't have that sell pressure.
The dividends and other debt obligations are something like 2-3b per year, and the reality is that the market has enough liquidity to pull that type of money and top up the cash reserve in a week of selling. Sure, there will be panic after he announces what he did, but any time they do this it will give them another year of runway to sit out the bear market.
If the bear market takes more than 2 or 3 years the BTC thesis is probably broken, but the fundamental treasury company assumption is that BTC will do OK in the future. Don't touch them if you think BTC will fail completely.

62406363
>>62405528
how is this similar to LUNA? mstr can stomach a few years of low price bitcoin without major issues.

>>62406041
why would they need to sell anything? The only real negative thesis is that bitcoin is dead. and i don't think so but it could be yeah

62406565
>>62406363
>why would they need to sell anything?
They have to pay dividends and convertible debt.
Per their website they have ~1.7b in annual dividend obligations now. They'll have to raise them for the $100 target, but they can do this slowly, so it may not change much.
They have a total of 6.7b in outstanding convertible debt, which assuming it doesn't hit the conversion price due to a terrible bear market, will mature as follows:
1b on Sep 15 2027
2b on Mar 1 2028
1.5b on Jun 1 2028
1.4b on Sep 15 2028
0.8b on Jun 15 2029

Their current cash reserve covers the dividend for nearly one year. So the total open obligations for the next years will be:
2026: $0, already covered by the reserve
2027: 0.85b div + 1b debt = ~1.85b
2028: 1.7b div + 4.9b debt = 6.6b
2029: 1.7b div + 0.8b debt = 2.5b
total = 10.9b

If we're still in bear market in early 2027, they might bite the bullet and sell 2-3b worth of BTC in a single week, causing panic but getting runway to early 2028.
If we're still in a bear market in early 2028, they might do another 2-3b BTC sale.
If we're still in a bear market in early 2029, they might do another 2-3b BTC sale.
Given that they pulled $300m from MSTR dilution just last week, it seems highly likely that they can pull at least 2-3b from dilution within the next 3 years even if the market sucks.
Using very conservative assumptions this would yield 8-12b cash to cover 11b in obligations.
They currently hold 50b worth of BTC. You can cut the current price in half and they can still stomach 3 years of bear market with plenty BTC left.

62406573
>>62405650
People were dumping their entire pensions into that garbage because the $100 peg was "so safe", lmao

62406600
>>62406041
I think they'll do what you're saying. Maybe sell off between $5-10B worth in one go to secure dividend funding and announce a lock-up on the remaining Bitcoin with a set number of years to try to reduce panic selling once it becomes public

62406601
>>62406041
>>62406565
Saylor is a retard and his ponzi is holding crypto back. Yes likely he is fine and it will collapse only 2029 but this is so retarded

62406637
>>62406363
10x worse, Luna didn't cause a massive selloff of BTC. The problem with impacting the main asset of a whole market is that it will spread to the market as whole. Luna already showed this in a smaller proportion. Expect many protocols to depeg and break.

62406662
>>62406601
Holding crypto back from what?

62406670
>>62406662
From number going up

62406739
>>62406565
He might as well dillute his shares to a single digit, what will happen is literally a death spiral, the market will stay away from MSTR and creditors might as well stop lending him money at low interest due to either a drop in his company rating and general macroeconomic data (rate hikes).

Also, the problem with STRC depegging and not being able to get back to $100 is that the interest he will be paying will start to exponentially rise. The basis was 11.5% apy, right now it's sitting at 14%. Those 3.5% is a big difference in his balance sheet, if STRC reaches $60, the interest is going to reach 19%, $50 is 23%. So you see where this leads to? If at some point it get worringly at $20, you are talking about 57.5% of annual, semi-monthly dividends to distribute. If he has cash for 2 years as of today, that means he would have left only a fourth of that amount, so 6 months AND then the obligations from his debts.

Anything that yields a really high disproportionate return relative to the whole market is really looked frowned upon by the market and investors, this is literally a ponzi and a death spiral. This could ultimately lead him to sell a portion of BTC still this year, and that could impact the market pretty badly, for instance, just see how the market reacts to any news of Bitcoin from MtGox that is retrieved and sent to exchanges., and that's a fraction of what Saylor has.

62406788
>>62406739
Typical bogus assumptions, like nearly every anti-Strategy post.
The STR products are very defensive. The dividend is voluntary. They can and will defend it, failing to do so would be a PR nightmare, but they are not legally obligated to run their entire company and the entire BTC market into the ground to defend it.
If it falls to $20, they can buy back that debt at a 80% discount and retire the dividend. That's a great deal.
Per the official prospectus of this product they also have an option to buy it back at par ($100), so there is no requirement to pay absurdly high dividends eternally if it turns out that this isn't working.
Outside of this option they can also start buybacks below par to retire that debt at a discount.
The basic sanity check you need is this:
BTC holdings: 50b
Debt (STR* + convertible): 22b

The convertible debt will take 3 years and is only 6.7b. The STR debt is designed to be spaced out over many more years, and they can suspend it as they see fit.

If BTC gets cut in half or worse and goes into a 10 year bear market, this thing isn't going to work out, but in that case who even cares for any treasury company anyway? We will have moved on to other things.

62406800
>>62405528
I lost all my money longing the way down already

62406801
>>62406565
that's good to know but dividends are optional. If things are really bad they can just not pay them rather than dump a lot of btc.

The fact that they have enough cash for 1 year of dividend is good.

>>62406637
Luna was worse. it was a stable coin pegged to a shitcoin. This isn't really the same thing. People can dump all their mstr and it goes to 0$ and btc will keep its value. The opposite isn't true however. Unless i'm missing something?

>>62406739
Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$? Yeah ok then maybe you have a point. all of this confusing, i looked it up and it says STRC is a preferrential share? these holders come first for dividends.
You're fudding me out of buying mstr below 50$ as i planned

62406840
>>62405528
gonna buy strc at $10 and make 115% a year

62406848
>>62406788
> bogus
Stop shitting the board with your claude slop posts. Fag.

62406888
>>62406637
>We're now in the "total cataclysm imminent" phase of the bear market

Holy shit I'm buying

62406891
>>62406801
He's bullshitting. If the price of STRC is $100 they're paying $11.50/share/year. If the price is $50 they're still paying $11.50/share/year. Unless they crank up dividends to try to maintain $100/share, but they're not obligated to, and it makes way more sense to just buyback shares at a discount, retire that obligation, wait for Bitcoin to drag itself along, and then start selling STRC at $100 again.

62406897
>>62406848
Claude is amazing, thank you for the compliment. My posts are completely handwritten. English isn't even my native language. I guess the coolest high schoolers nowadays don't say "bogus" and will remember this in the future.
I only clarified the meaning of the "Earliest Call Date" on https://www.strategy.com/credit using AI (not Claude).
>>62406788
>Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$?
STRC is not pegged. It has a $100 target price controlled by the dividend. There's a set of rules to respond to prices too far below $100 by raising the dividend incrementally, thereby hopefully raising it to par eventually.
What naysayers also miss is that every $100 share was sold for the promise to pay $11.5 per year. If you want your $100 back, you have to sell into the open market. This is why the BTC bought using STR gives them a long runway.
As stated above, if the whole thing doesn't work out, they have the right to buy all of it back at $100.

62406933
>>62405528
saylorcoin is fucked till hes out

62406934
>>62406788
You got a point. He can rebuy but the dividends will accrue with interest, he will need to pay someday.

His bitcoin holdings are 50b now, at the money, we are expecting bitcoin to drop at least 30% further and hit 40k, so that will get closer and closer to his debt. From experience and seeing what normies are saying, they are also claiming 40k so I expect going even lower.

>>62406801

I don't want to fud I want to buy MSTR to have an extra exposure, it's interesting to buy at really low prices. He might not go bankrupt, but I would pay attention to his company until this bear ends.

62406939
>>62406801
See >>62406897 (wrongly replied to myself).
Also, the real risks are that dividend payments from STR products can be suspended as they see fit, and there is no requirement to protect the principal at any cost.
This is why the death spiral fantasies of Strategy running itself and BTC into the ground are unrealistic. They don't have to do this, so they won't.
However, rugpulling STR products, which they could do in theory, would hurt their credit line, stock price, etc. If you check the STRC holders and say Yahoo Finance you'll see that BlackRock, VanEck and Fidelity ETFs put hundreds of millions into this. Strategy is unlikely to rugpull them.
Realistically they can easily sit out a bear market for multiple years, they'll easily be able to pay the dividends and make some effort to get back to $100 par, but you can't depend on this especially in the near term.

62406959
Where Saylor get the money? There must be some glitch.. he keep buying at loss and pay High interest but he don't produce nothing useful. That's like a life cheat code, the ultimate Ponzi materialization

62406974
>>62406959
he just needs to increase the strc yield to 35%

item.title: Binance out of the EU? — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Binance out of the EU?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:11 UTC

OP

How is this going to affect the crypto market?

Early replies (first 3)

62404563
>>62404556
>the crypto market
what?

you thought theres still a market?

lol

why you think every pump gets sold off?

underwater MMs are slowly offloading to $0 into ever-decreasing demand thats your market

62404564
>>62404556
why are you still talking about crypto even? Everyone with a brain has moved onto stocks

62404566
>>62404563
MMs? Market makers? Dont those cunts make their money out of the spread?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404567
>>62404564
I still have a bunch of usdt and there are things like staking, gold stablecoins and spacex gambling.

62404608
>>62404556
peepoo ll hold their own coins and slimy cz wont be able to manipulate the prices so easily.

62404611
>>62404556
see how dast the lil droids sliding the thred, it fears :D

62404615
>>62404556
Anyone here still using binance?

62404622
>>62404556
What kind of idiot uses Binance?

62404679
>>62404622
It seems that there are a lot of idiots anon, is the biggest exchange in the world.

62404688
>>62404622
Whats the better alternative? And please dont tell mot robinhood or some shit

62404733
>>62404556
That could cause a dump

62404795
>>62404679
They spoof volume, if they had the same regs they'd be smaller than coinbase

62404802
>>62404795
They operate in europe so they have the same regulations than coinbase, they are the biggest one because they operate on the most regions

62404814
>>62404802
This thread is about them not operating in Europe anymore because they dont meet the regs

62404825
>>62404814
The things is that this could cause a massive withdrawal, do they really have your coins in there?

62404832
>>62404814
Well they still are, no official statement was issued to users yet

62404835
>>62404825
Im not too worried about it, its been very telegraphed and binance has handled stuff like this before without much issue

Its much more lucrative for them to keep collecting fees rather than rug

62404874
>>62404688
Dexes. Only two limitations come to my mind: 1) can be hard/expensive to trade fiat->crypto and 2) you can't get "physical delivery" of dumb chain coins like BTC or LTC that don't have trustless bridges to smart chains. For everything else dexes are just better.

62405781
>>62404556
it's only temporary as long as they don't have their licence

62405795
>>62404874
dexes suck, only order books matter for trading and the volume is low, exchanges have more funds to get, that's without accounting the fact that they rely on crypto which is not needed, crypto is not replacing centralized servers

62405831
>>62404556
Europeans are poor, hence europoor. They barely have money to invest. Sometimes I think I'm blessed that my German Borderline ex-wife divorced me, imagine living in a country where you barely can make ends meet AND life alongside jeets, sandniggers and kikes that sabotage a whole continent.

62405835
>>62405831
Just so sum up:
>How is this going to affect the crypto market?
It won't.

62405842
CZ will finally use his Samson Option.
October 10th was just the appetizer.

62406979
>>62405795
>only order books matter
You are only thinking about AMMs. Every perp dex is order book.
>the volume is low
Hyperliquid has literally higher volume than Coinbase.
>exchanges have more funds to get
No clue what you meant by this.
>they rely on crypto which is not needed
One is leaving EU because of regulation while the other stays accessible globally without KYC. You tell me which one is replacing which.

item.title: Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP." — 50 replies — 83.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP."

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-24 18:38 UTC

OP

Sergey says: "Just use Chainlink CCIP."

Early replies (first 3)

62388705
>>62388673
Loving his use of hamburger grease in his hair.

62388723
I have been seeing this creature for years now but I never really stopped to take a closer look. I regret doing that today as every feature of this unfortunate man is completly appalling.

62388769
>>62388723

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393653
>>62393645
Everything else i look at is so pumped already. Everyone's portfolio i look at is full of AI shit. I already have exposure to all that noise. Chainlink and Gamestop are my only holdings that aren't pumped up like crazy right now. Chainlink in particular is due for a good rebound

62393746
>>62393595
I've been loading my HSA with glnk, so yeah go for it anon.

62393862
>>62393653

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 40 replies — 70.1/hr

Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-06-24 18:39 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400285
>>62399898

62401259

62402158

62402616

62402679

62403095

62404122

62404487

62405784

62405818

item.title: Why is canada a bad investment? — 39 replies — 36.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is canada a bad investment?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-24 18:10 UTC

OP

Even though Canada's population is skyrocketing and GDP is skyrocketing, the stocks and houses are slow.
What's up with that?

Early replies (first 3)

62401828
Lol

62401832
>>62401803
would you invest in India? no? well there is your answer

62401837
>>62401803

a fucking LEAF.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401939
>>62401803
Most of the economy is just people trading empty shitboxes back and forth.

62401960
>>62401803
>lies lies lies lies
Shut the fuck up and get in the MAID chamber.

62402066
>>62401803
hopefully there's a civil war with alberta that causes all the dots and chinks to flee back to their home countries

62402168
Here's how pathetic canada is, their citizens rent mailboxes in the US so they can online shop with US prices on the American internet.

62402178
people were buying 300k houses for 850k so now it needs to regulate

62402181
Its all the bad parts of america and all the bad parts of Europe slapped together

62402189
>Eurotard type taxes

>Sky high real estate costs

> Essentials are fucking expensive due to oligarchs

>Too may immigrants

Only profit makers are boomers who brought many homes too early.

62402244
>>62402168
I just moved to Murica and get free rent in Murica.

62403424
>>62401861
The Canadian Shield will never be populated in any significant manner, no matter how warmer it gets.
BC is too mountainous.
The middle provinces are no different than Nebraska if they warm up.
It really comes down to Toronto, and the Windsor-Quebec corridor in general. NIMBYism, boomers, and general incompetence will make sure it never develops meaningfully.
Canada can support 100M people at most, which is respectable but will never be USA-tier. Their economy will also not scale linearly with their population as we can clearly see now. Their GDP per capita will crater.

62403439
>>62403424
All my calculations and peer reviewed journals say canada can indeed support 400 million people

62403445
>>62403424
If nigeria can support 250 million people, then canada can easily match that, since we have more than double their arable land

62403479
>>62403439
>>62403445
Real life isn't Sim City. Sure you can theoretically reach those numbers, but your quality of life will drop so hard that immigrants will no longer be attracted to come over.

62403519
>>62401803
Because Canada is in the middle of a rather rapid transition from a white country to a brown country. There's no long term upside. It is quite literally over.

62403732
>>62401803
Did you check the news dumb nigger? Its India 2.0 now?

62403757
>>62401803
Leaf here
I keep all my money in USD and crypto. We're headed for an Argentina style collapse and it won't even take that long

62405658
>>62403757
Why usd, the dollar is toilet paper

62405670
>>62402181
Yup. We work like Americans to get paid like Europeans (not quite that bad but still)

62405677
>>62401860
Canadians are fucking filthy

62405699
>>62401803
Canadas main product now is scams and government paperwork. 25% of the population either works directly or indirectly for the government. Manufacturing is dead, resource extraction is dead, housing is imploding from a two decade long bubble. The only tool the government wants to use is to increase taxes, scammers and paperwork.

It can still be saved though, whoever owns canada just has to be willing to do it.

62405735
>moving to Canada this Winter
>mfw reading this thread

62405870
>>62405735
in which city are you moving

62405898
>>62402066
Won't happen, they'd rather die than go back. Only way they will is with guns in their backs and a bullet in the head of any that try to fight back.

62405903

62406388
>>62405870

>>62405903

item.title: USA vs Germany drug pricing conflict — 36 replies — 26.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
USA vs Germany drug pricing conflict

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-24 17:52 UTC

OP

>The United States has launched a trade investigation into Germany
over its plans to cut spending on pharmaceuticals.
>U.S. Trade Representative Jameson Greer says the probe is looking into
whether Berlin's drug pricing policies are unfair or discriminatory towards American companies.
>The investigation comes under Section 301 of the U.S. Trade Act
and could lead to tariffs on German exports if Washington finds evidence of unfair practice.
>Germany is facing growing financial pressure on its public health care system
with a projected budget shortfall of around €20 billion.
>To help control the cost, Berlin has proposed measures that would require pharmaceutical companies
to offer larger discounts on expensive new drugs.
>However, the Trump administration argues that such policies allow countries like Germany
to access medicines at much lower prices,
>forcing American consumers to shoulder a disproportionate share of the cost of developing new medicines.
Just two months ago, Washington used these exact same trade threats
>to pressure the UK into a historic deal,
forcing the British government to effectively double its spending on new medicines
>to avoid American tariffs.

https://www.dailymotion.com/video/xah8e3m

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/19/us-tariff-probe-germany-drug-pricing-policies-mfn.html

https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/us-launches-section-301-probe-into-germany-over-drug-pricing-2026-06-19/

https://www.euronews.com/business/2026/06/19/us-launches-trade-probe-into-germany-over-medicine-prices

Early replies (first 3)

62404118
>>62404112
pretty sure the SCOTUS ruled most of trump's tariffs illegal

also pretty sure germany knows this

62404127
>>62404118
>Just two months ago, Washington used these exact same trade threats
>to pressure the UK into a historic deal,
>forcing the British government to effectively double its spending on new medicines
>to avoid American tariffs.

62404280
>>62404127
But cant Germany rely on the EU therefore saving itself?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404320
I posted some stuff in these threads. Hopefully someone finds the parallel I'm drawing to the fish and wine conflict interesting, which AI agreed with and gave a good elaboration on, see picrel and these threads.

>>>/news/1520712

>>>/pol/537508422

I think this is about the state using business as a power tool, just like The East India Company and The United Fruit Company. Hopefully we can discuss that.

62404327
>>62404112
Why do you post like a fuckin sped?

62404333
>>62404320
I cropped out the irrelevant parts.

62404338
>>62404112
american pharma shareholder jew wants more shekels

62404343
>>62404112
good, yuropoors are gonna pay up

62404346
>>62404338
>>62404343
Brainlet discussion

62404364
>>62404333
>>62404346
Ok but how is this 100 year old paper relevant?

62404366
>>62404118
Trump can just use military aid to Ukraine as leverage to force the EU countries to do anything he wants

62404369
>>62404112
Kek i have this feeling that soon the USA will start ignoring patents and allow vsrious things to be copied without being able to get sued by foreign companies

62404383
>yuropoors think they're allowed to lower the price of their medicine
LOL

62404425
>>62404364
>Frito Pendejo stare

62404430
>>62404383
I was hoping for actual discussion.

62404440
>>62404112
The government is a client just like any other, but because it's a bigger client than a bunch of shitty hospitals they have more negotiation power and get better deals. I find it amusing braindead drumbers who run their mouth about free markets and business all day can't understand something so basic.

62404448
>>62404320
It's about the american government acting retarded. Using tariffs to damage strategic competitors like china would be a good use of them. But if you really are concerned about trade deficits, as they claim they are, all you need to do is stop deficit spending and pretending all that foreign money you're borrowing goes nowhere.
In this particular case they have this idea that pharma companies charge americans more because europoors pay less, and not because their prices are negotiated by weak fragmented players that take kickbacks. If prices in europe go up, the profit margins of the pharma companies go up, and americans keep paying the same because they have a lump of fat in the place where you'd expect to find their brain.

62404459
>>62404440
Yeah but the US government is threatening with tariffs on German goods if they put a lower price. Shoving the medicines down their throat.
>No you can't have these drugs for $10, pay $20 or we'll buy fewer of your BMWs.
As I said it's exactly like the fish and wine conflict.
>we don't want to buy your wine
>then we won't buy your fish
>ok, just kidding, we want to buy your wine

62404462
>>62404455
I reckon it's more about a bunch of poor and starving pharma executives showing up in the white house (or the office of one of the illiterate clowns he has for ministers) and crying that the evil german government was trying to take their lunch money, and because drumber is such a charitable soul and hates the german chancelor because he called him a pathetic loser that got beaten up by a bunch of ragheads in iran, he decided to take up their cause, dog bless his soul

62404464
>>62404448
They say the motive is that Americans pay more for drugs, yes. But I think that might just be rhetoric to get the American populace to feel that it's right. I think it goes deeper. What politicians say they're doing and what they're actually doing are rarely the same thing.

62404468
>>62404464
refer to>>62404462
they got lobbied to cuck germany, it really is that simple with these people

62404470
>>62404280
lmao

62404480
>>62404468
I think there was lobbying yes but I think these things are a two-way street. Copying a post I posted in the /pol/ thread:

this is about the state using corporations as a power tool. Same thing that happened with The East India Company and The United Fruit Company. Business (corporations) is the engine of the state. I think the alcohol situation I mentioned a hundred years ago is a perfect analogy for what's going on. Imagine a country has a lot of vineyards. One country is trying to ban alcohol. The first country imposes tariffs in retaliation. Then the wine begins flowing again. The wine export brings money into the state, even if the vineyards are privately owned. This is how corporations and the state are two sides of the same coin. The state uses the vineyard as a power tool, the vineyard uses the state to get the wine sold, a two-way street. That's the same thing with this situation. Germany wants to keep drug prices low, like Norway wanted to limit alcohol consumption. The exporting country wants to increase their own wealth and power. Same dynamic.

62404484
Are we finally learning that 'free healthcare' isn't really free?

62404496
>>62404484
in my country we get taxed heavily, to pay for 'free' health care, but it wasn't enough, so the government introduced a 'temporary' levy in 1984 (now 2% of your income) to pay for said 'free' health care and then this still wasn't enough so a 'temporary' surcharge was introduced in 1997, additional to the 'temporary' levy, for those who could afford private health insurance but did not have it.

62404504
>>62404112
who the fuck cares what germans have to say

62404509
>>62404496
oh I forgot to mention that the premiums for private health care are greater than the 'temporary' surcharge so people are opting to pay it instead of getting private health insurance and now the entire insurance industry is at risk of collapsing due to the 5.5 million boomers (21.5% of the total population) all starting to require increasing health care at the same time and not enough people paying into the private health system to cover the ever increasing costs.

62404520
>>62404366
He has already dumped the costs of supporting Ukraine onto European countries. There is virtually nothing more to cut, unless he wants to cut C4ISR support and thus throw the war. Despite the impression some people get from his public rhetoric, he doesn't want to do that.

62404533
>>62404468
>>62404464
>>62404480

It's not just corporations. It's the entirety of the pharma industry. You have
-Private hospitals
-Health insurance companies
-Pharmaceutical companies
They're all working together to squeeze the average US citizen for as much cash as possible. That's why you have insane prices for a single bandaid in a hospital.

By lobbying and pushing for higher prices on pharmaceuticals in Germany or the entire EU, the government won't be able to keep up the free healthcare thing (which is already unsustainable btw) and force people to resort to private healthcare (insurances/hospitals which is already somewhat a thing in EU). Once that happens it will facilitate
-More private healthcare/insurance companies
-Higher cost of treatments
-Bigger profit margins for pharma companies
It's not just about allowing some pharma companies to charge more, it's about trying to spread the infection that is the private healthcare system in the US to the EU, a largely untapped market. It's painfully obvious.

62404539
>we dont have money
>Lets force them to sell it for cheaper
Its that easy huh?

62404552
>>62404533
>muh one-way street
Didn't read because you didn't read jack shit of my posts.

62404562
>>62404484
More like, the free market is bullshit, and has always been.

62406464
bump

item.title: I just opened up a $768,000,000 short on btc at $63000 We are headed down to $13000 now you've been warned. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I just opened up a $768,000,000 short on btc at $63000 We are headed down to $13000 now you've been warned.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:13 UTC

OP

I just opened up a $768,000,000 short on btc at $63000 We are headed down to $13000 now you've been warned. People like me are winners. We never lose. I've been in this space since 2011 and have made more money than this entire board combined.

I got into bitcoin when the only time to obtain it was through mining on a laptop on bitcoin talk forums
I Invested into the ethereum ICO at $0.40
Myself and my team of whales i work with pumped all the 2017 coins - tron , verge, omisego etcetc We were also responsible for mooning link up in the bear market and alot of the defi 2020 summer. We also pumped all of the layer 1's this market cycle like sol/luna/avax/one/ftm We literally control this entire market and make it go up and down which ever way we see fit. We harvested billions out of pleb retail newfags this market cycle and I am telling you right now that its all over.

I know the people who are behind the curtains and control this space. I and the people i know in this space create the tops and the bottoms. We run this market. Without guys like me you wouldn't have any pumps happen on any of your alts. So listen up faggots. Enter into a short like me with the big money and win. We are going to $13000 and maybe even down to $8800. You've been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62403725
Is this some kind of pithy reverse psychology shit
Look dude I'm not investing fucking anything into a currency that requires electricity to exist. No.

62403729
>>62403725
Newfag bot. Also this pasta needs shirtless men not a jeet

62403761
>>62403729
Ye I only trust gaywhaleanon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404752
>>62403725
your life requires electricity to exist faggot

62406782
What are the odds that OP is correct & giving us the secret knowledge? I am about to put a 50k short on btc after getting a loan for my "business" kek

62406998
>>62403720
>I know the people who are behind the curtains and control this space

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405345
>>62405324
hes fuuuucked

62405465
So when does he get liquidated?

62405479
>>62405465
He doesn't. He can always just refinance the debt.

62405485
>>62405479
Bummer. I want carnage

62405492
>>62405479
refinance where michael?

62405495
>>62405479

62405524
the MSTR reddit is full of laughs today

62405532
>>62405324
Luna 2.0

62405536
>>62405334
So there's still one intelligent person on /biz/ other than myself. That's nice.

62405576
>>62405334
>>62405536
Buy in October after BTC bottom. I was foreseeing his stocks to go $80, but by the way things are going, I see this dipping to $60, perhaps lower.

Honestly, I doubt if there will ever get a recovery, depends on how everything pans out. He can't sell STRC to pay dividends, he's dumping his MSTR shares to start paying dividends and buying bitcoin. Soon there will be rate hikes and lending and refinancing will start to get expensive. Institutions could even stop refinancing and lending him money as his company rating is low and could even lower if he keeps selling shares and, in the worst case, doesn't pay the dividends (which in my opinion he did something terrible changing to semi-monthly).

62405874
>>62405324

62406361
>>62405333
There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen.

62406664
>>62405479

62406710
>>62405479
How is that possible? He is obviously abusing some kind of bug in the finance system to have infinite money, how can that be legal?

item.title: YOU DONT DESERVE TO LIVE AN UNCOMFORTABLE LIF....AAAAAACK — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
YOU DONT DESERVE TO LIVE AN UNCOMFORTABLE LIF....AAAAAACK

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:13 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62406658
The thing is, if they raise the dividend or whatever, people have already shown they don't want to buy when it's 90 dollars, or hell even 80 dollars. Raising the dividend would only ever bring it back to the lowest price. The market already said it wasn't interested at 100-80$.

62406706
>>62406644
Michael
I was promised 11% returns
Where is my money??????

62406941
>>62406644
saylorcoin is fucked till hes out

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62406954
>>62406644
When the first dividend missed......

62406992
>hike the dividend
>strc goes up
>refinance the higher debt obligations
ezpz but the faggot's silence is deafening atm

62406460

Lower your goal to $1k or maybe $100, and then you'll have my respect.
